extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked 45 seconds behind with 31km to go, bridged with Vingegaard, then won stage from elite five-man finale.
tactical_patterns: Pogačar attacked from deficit position at 31km out requiring Vingegaard to chase, flipping usual Tour dynamic where yellow jersey defends.
Adam Yates executed leadout for Pogačar final kilometer, abandoning own stage ambitions to secure UAE teammate's morale win.
Simon Yates attacked from chase group during final climb, gaining GC position over Carlos Rodriguez through speculative late move.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: still attacking from 7+ minutes down, suggesting psychological need for stage win outweighs energy conservation for future years
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: marked Pogačar's attack but showed no interest in contesting stage, prioritizing GC security over marginal gains
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: sacrificed stage opportunity to lead out Pogačar, indicating UAE's team cohesion remains intact despite GC defeat
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: attacked independently from chase group while brother worked for Pogačar, moving to fourth GC through late-race opportunism
rider_signals.GALL Felix: negative: despite being close in KOM standings, couldn't follow final elite group suggesting single-climb specialist vulnerability
rider_signals.GAUDU David: negative: crashed out of top-10 contention on penultimate stage, revealing fragility for podium ambitions in future Grand Tours
